questions.original_question: What is Ningbo Viridi, and what is Zeekr’s ownership in the company?
questions.answer:
Ningbo Viridi is a subsidiary primarily engaged in the research, development, and production of key components for new energy vehicles (NEVs), including battery packs, motors, electric control systems, and charging solutions. Founded in 2017 as an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Geely Holding, it became a significant part of Lotus Technology’s operations after Lotus Technology acquired a 51% equity interest in October 2021. Ningbo Viridi’s innovations include high-performance batteries, such as the self-developed Golden Battery, which offers rapid charging capabilities, and advanced battery management systems (BMS) that meet stringent safety standards. The company also develops motor and electric control products, contributing to the efficiency and performance of vehicles like the Zeekr series. Additionally, Ningbo Viridi provides charging solutions, including ultra-fast charging piles, and operates a large manufacturing facility in Ningbo, China. It plays a crucial role in Lotus Technology’s revenue generation and R&D efforts, supplying products to both Geely Group and Lotus Technology. 

Regarding ownership, Zeekr holds a 51% equity interest in Ningbo Viridi, with the remaining 49% retained by Geely Holding. This ownership structure was established following the completion of the acquisition in October 2021. The information is based on knowledge available before 2024-12-31.

rag_info.chunk_content:
(1) In July 2021, ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., or ZEEKR Shanghai, acquired 100% equity interest in ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., or ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay, from Geely Holding. In August 2021, ZEEKR Shanghai acquired 100% equity interest in ZEEKR Shanghai (99% from Geely Auto and 1% from Geely Holding).  
(2) Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., or Ningbo Viridi, was founded in 2017 as an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Geely Holding and is engaged in the R&D and production of key NEV components, including electric powertrains and battery packs. In July 2021, ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. entered into a share purchase agreement with Ningbo Viridi and Zhejiang Jichuang Industrial Development Co., Ltd., pursuant to which ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. agreed to purchase a 51% equity interest in Ningbo Viridi. In October 2021, the acquisition was completed. Currently, Ningbo Viridi is owned as to 51% by ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. and 49% by Geely Holding. Ningbo Viridi started to generate revenue in March 2019.

Ningbo Viridi innovates and pioneers a series of battery products and solutions for battery electric vehicles (BEVs), hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs), and plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs), featuring a high level of energy, convenience, and safety. For instance, Ningbo Viridi's batteries are onboard vehicles with a CLTC range of 546 km to 806 km. Ningbo Viridi has commercially released its self-developed Golden Battery, which can be replenished from 10% to 80% within approximately 10.5 minutes. This achievement enables lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries to surpass the charge rates of ternary lithium batteries. In addition, Ningbo Viridi has developed the battery management system (BMS) to ensure performance, efficiency, and durability of battery packs. Based on AutoSar architecture and ISO26262, Ningbo Viridi’s BMS achieves the safety standard of ASIL C. Ningbo Viridi’s batteries and BMS have been deployed on a number of BEV brands within Geely Group. Motor and electric control. Ningbo Viridi is devoted to the research and development of motor and electric control products that offer high performance and efficiency, as well as low weight and improved noise, vibration, and harshness (NVH). The motor with a peak power of 310 kW is equipped in the new generation of Zeekr 009, Zeekr 001, Zeekr 7X, and other vehicle brands. Charging solutions. Ningbo Viridi’s charging solution offers onboard charger solutions and charging pile solutions, such as the up-to-800 kW charging piles deployed in ultra charging stations.

Ningbo Viridi is one of Lotus Technology's subsidiaries that generates revenue by implementing the production, sales, and after-sales services relating to battery packs, motors, electric control systems, charging solutions, and energy storage products and systems. Lotus Technology controls a 51% equity interest in Ningbo Viridi. For details of Lotus Technology's equity interests in Ningbo Viridi, see “Our History and Corporate Structure.” The product portfolio of Ningbo Viridi includes the products and systems of (i) battery packs, (ii) motor and electric control systems, (iii) charging solutions, and (iv) energy storage products. Ningbo Viridi has a facility in Ningbo, China with a gross floor area of approximately 226,577 square meters, where Ningbo Viridi conducts product manufacturing and testing. Ningbo Viridi hosts three production lines, each for battery packs, motor and electric control products, and its proprietary battery management system, or BMS, products. Ningbo Viridi mainly supplies the products to vehicle brands inside Geely Group and Lotus Technology. In 2021, 2022, and 2023, the revenues generated from the business operations of Ningbo Viridi amounted to RMB2,385.7 million, RMB10,391.8 million, and RMB15,268.3 million (US$2,150.5 million), respectively, representing 36.5%, 32.6%, and 29.5% of Lotus Technology's total revenue for the same periods, respectively. Ningbo Viridi is also a key R&D center for Lotus Technology. For a detailed discussion of the research and development activities conducted by Ningbo Viridi, see “— Research and Development — Ningbo Viridi.”
Currently, Ningbo Viridi is owned as to 51% by Lotus Technology and 49% by Geely Holding. Ningbo Viridi started to generate revenue in March 2019.

rag_info.chunk_content: In October 2021, the Group through ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. acquired 51% equity interest in Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., a subsidiary of Geely Holding, by injecting capital contribution of RMB860.7 million in Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd. As a result of this transaction, Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd. became a subsidiary of the Group with a 49% non-controlling interest in the amount of RMB743,088 recognized at the date of acquisition. In February 2022, the Group through Zhejiang Zeekr acquired 100% equity interest in ZEEKR Technology Europe AB from Geely Holding for a cash consideration of Swedish Krona (“SEK”) 1,057.8 million (RMB741.3 million). The net book value of ZEEKR Technology Europe AB was RMB728.1 million at the time of acquisition. The corresponding difference between acquisition consideration paid and the entity’s net book value of RMB13,130 loss was recognized in additional paid-in capital. Upon the completion of the above transactions, ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., and ZEEKR Technology Europe AB became operating subsidiaries of the Group. In May 2024, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited successfully listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE).

metadata.title_summary:
title: Ningbo Viridi 
summary: Ningbo Viridi, a subsidiary in which we hold a 51% equity interest, specializes in the production, sales, and aftersales services of battery packs, motors, electric controls, charging solutions, and energy storage systems. Strategically located manufacturing bases across China enable the company to support a diverse product portfolio in four key technology sectors. In 2022, 2023, and 2024, Ningbo Viridi generated revenues of RMB 10,391.8 million, RMB 15,268.3 million, and RMB 17,029.5 million (approximately US$ 2,333.0 million), accounting for 32.6%, 29.5%, and 22.4% of our total revenue, respectively.

The company serves primarily vehicle brands within the Geely Group and acts as a vital research and development hub, focusing on innovation in battery technology for Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs), Hybrid Electric Vehicles (HEVs), and Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicles (PHEVs). Notably, Ningbo Viridi has developed the self-designed Golden Battery, which charges from 10% to 80% in about 10.5 minutes and offers a CLTC range of 546 km to 806 km, surpassing traditional battery technologies. Additionally, their Battery Management System (BMS) meets AutoSar architecture and ISO26262 standards, achieving ASIL C safety certification.

Beyond battery solutions, Ningbo Viridi produces high-performance motors, including a 310 kW peak power motor used in the latest Zeekr models, and offers advanced charging solutions such as on-board chargers and high-capacity charging piles capable of delivering up to 800 kW. As of December 31, 2024, the company employs 1,922 professionals with expertise in automotive and mechanical fields, reinforcing its commitment to excellence in electric vehicle technology. For further details on ownership and R&D activities, please refer to the relevant sections in our company documentation.

title: Corporate Structure 
summary: The corporate structure of the organization, as outlined in the prospectus, illustrates a hierarchical framework led by a parent company based in the Cayman Islands, which oversees a network of both offshore and onshore subsidiaries, particularly in the New Energy Vehicles (NEV) sector. The diagram highlights key ownership percentages, geographic locations, and the interrelationships among various entities.

At the top of the structure is the Cayman Islands-based parent company, which fully owns ZEEKR Technology Innovation Limited in the British Virgin Islands. This entity, in turn, wholly owns ZEEKR Technology Limited in Hong Kong, which marks the transition to onshore operations through its complete ownership of Zhejiang ZEEKR. 

Zhejiang ZEEKR further extends its reach by owning several subsidiaries, including CEVT, a wholly owned Swedish subsidiary specializing in software systems and engineering R&D for NEVs, acquired in February 2022. Additionally, ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. owns ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., which was acquired in July 2021.

The organization also engages in joint ventures, such as Ningbo Viridi, where it holds a 51% stake alongside Geely Holding, focusing on electric powertrains and battery production since generating revenue in March 2019. Furthermore, Zhejiang ZEEKR owns three additional subsidiaries dedicated to various operational functions related to NEVs, including research and development, sales, and technology services.

Overall, the corporate structure underscores the company's strategic focus on NEV technology development, showcasing significant acquisitions and ownership stakes while distinguishing between offshore and onshore entities.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Ningbo Viridi 
summary: Ningbo Viridi, a subsidiary in which we hold a 51% equity interest, specializes in the production, sales, and aftersales services of battery packs, motors, electric controls, charging solutions, and energy storage systems. Strategically located manufacturing bases across China enable the company to support a diverse product portfolio in four key technology sectors. In 2022, 2023, and 2024, Ningbo Viridi generated revenues of RMB 10,391.8 million, RMB 15,268.3 million, and RMB 17,029.5 million (approximately US$ 2,333.0 million), accounting for 32.6%, 29.5%, and 22.4% of our total revenue, respectively.

The company serves primarily vehicle brands within the Geely Group and acts as a vital research and development hub, focusing on innovation in battery technology for Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs), Hybrid Electric Vehicles (HEVs), and Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicles (PHEVs). Notably, Ningbo Viridi has developed the self-designed Golden Battery, which charges from 10% to 80% in about 10.5 minutes and offers a CLTC range of 546 km to 806 km, surpassing traditional battery technologies. Additionally, their Battery Management System (BMS) meets AutoSar architecture and ISO26262 standards, achieving ASIL C safety certification.

Beyond battery solutions, Ningbo Viridi produces high-performance motors, including a 310 kW peak power motor used in the latest Zeekr models, and offers advanced charging solutions such as on-board chargers and high-capacity charging piles capable of delivering up to 800 kW. As of December 31, 2024, the company employs 1,922 professionals with expertise in automotive and mechanical fields, reinforcing its commitment to excellence in electric vehicle technology. For further details on ownership and R&D activities, please refer to the relevant sections in our company documentation.

Furthermore, Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., established in 2017 as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Geely Holding, focuses on R&D and production of essential NEV components, including electric powertrains and battery packs. In July 2021, ZEEKR entered a share purchase agreement to acquire a 51% equity interest in Ningbo Viridi, completing the transaction in October 2021. As a result, ZEEKR now holds a majority stake in Ningbo Viridi, which has been generating revenue since March 2019.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Corporate History 
summary: ZEEKR originated as a business unit within Geely Auto in October 2017 and has since evolved into a distinct entity with a focus on premium electric vehicles. Established as ZEEKR Intelligent Technology in March 2021 in the Cayman Islands, the company was co-founded by Mr. Shufu Li and others to pursue operational and financial independence from Geely Auto, allowing it to cultivate its own brand identity and attract diverse investors. The organization operates through several subsidiaries, including ZEEKR Automobile and ZEEKR Technology, and has made significant acquisitions to enhance its capabilities, such as acquiring ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay and CEVT from Geely Holding.

ZEEKR launched its first battery electric vehicle, the ZEEKR 001, in April 2021, followed by the ZEEKR 009 in November 2022 and the ZEEKR X compact SUV in April 2023. The company continues to expand its product lineup, introducing models like the ZEEKR 001 FR in October 2023 and an upscale sedan in January 2024. In April 2024, ZEEKR unveiled the ZEEKR 009 Grand, a luxury variant, and the ZEEKR MIX, a multi-purpose vehicle. This strategic focus on premium offerings and innovative designs positions ZEEKR as a competitive player in the electric vehicle market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Ningbo Viridi 
summary: Ningbo Viridi, a subsidiary in which we hold a 51% equity interest, specializes in the production, sales, and aftersales services of battery packs, motors, electric controls, charging solutions, and energy storage systems. Strategically located manufacturing bases across China enable the company to support a diverse product portfolio in four key technology sectors. In 2022, 2023, and 2024, Ningbo Viridi generated revenues of RMB 10,391.8 million, RMB 15,268.3 million, and RMB 17,029.5 million (approximately US$ 2,333.0 million), accounting for 32.6%, 29.5%, and 22.4% of our total revenue, respectively.

The company serves primarily vehicle brands within the Geely Group and acts as a vital research and development hub, focusing on innovation in battery technology for Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs), Hybrid Electric Vehicles (HEVs), and Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicles (PHEVs). Notably, Ningbo Viridi has developed the self-designed Golden Battery, which charges from 10% to 80% in about 10.5 minutes and offers a CLTC range of 546 km to 806 km, surpassing traditional battery technologies. Additionally, their Battery Management System (BMS) meets AutoSar architecture and ISO26262 standards, achieving ASIL C safety certification.

Beyond battery solutions, Ningbo Viridi produces high-performance motors, including a 310 kW peak power motor used in the latest Zeekr models, and offers advanced charging solutions such as on-board chargers and high-capacity charging piles capable of delivering up to 800 kW. As of December 31, 2024, the company employs 1,922 professionals with expertise in automotive and mechanical fields, reinforcing its commitment to excellence in electric vehicle technology. For further details on ownership and R&D activities, please refer to the relevant sections in our company documentation.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Ningbo Viridi innovates and pioneers a series of battery products and solutions for battery electric vehicles (BEVs), hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs), and plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs), featuring a high level of energy, convenience, and safety. For instance, Ningbo Viridi's batteries are onboard vehicles with a CLTC range of 546 km to 806 km. Ningbo Viridi has commercially released its self-developed Golden Battery, which can be replenished from 10% to 80% within approximately 10.5 minutes. This achievement enables lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries to surpass the charge rates of ternary lithium batteries. In addition, Ningbo Viridi has developed the battery management system (BMS) to ensure performance, efficiency, and durability of battery packs. Based on AutoSar architecture and ISO26262, Ningbo Viridi’s BMS achieves the safety standard of ASIL C. Ningbo Viridi’s batteries and BMS have been deployed on a number of BEV brands within Geely Group. Motor and electric control. Ningbo Viridi is devoted to the research and development of motor and electric control products that offer high performance and efficiency, as well as low weight and improved noise, vibration, and harshness (NVH). The motor with a peak power of 310 kW is equipped in the new generation of Zeekr 009, Zeekr 001, Zeekr 7X, and other vehicle brands. Charging solutions. Ningbo Viridi’s charging solution offers onboard charger solutions and charging pile solutions, such as the up-to-800 kW charging piles deployed in ultra charging stations.

title: Corporate Structure 
summary: The annual report features a diagram that illustrates the corporate structure of the organization, showcasing both significant and additional subsidiaries as mandated by Section 1-02 of Regulation S-X under the Securities Act. This representation is up-to-date as of the report's date.

At the apex of the hierarchy is the parent company, incorporated in the Cayman Islands, which fully owns ZEEKR Technology Innovation Limited, an offshore holding company based in the British Virgin Islands. This entity wholly owns ZEEKR Technology Limited in Hong Kong, marking the shift to onshore operations. ZEEKR Technology Limited, in turn, fully owns Zhejiang ZEEKR, which oversees two primary onshore subsidiaries: ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. and Zhejiang ZEEKR Automobile Research & Development Co., Ltd. The former holds a 51% stake in Ningbo Viridi, a company specializing in new energy vehicle (NEV) components, while the latter focuses on research and development.

The corporate structure is designed to delineate the various roles and responsibilities of its entities, with onshore subsidiaries managing domestic operations and offshore entities serving as intermediaries for ownership consolidation. Notable acquisitions include ZEEKR Automobile's purchase of ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay and the ownership structure of Ningbo Viridi, where ZEEKR owns 51% and Geely Holding retains 49%. Additionally, ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. acquired full ownership of ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd. from Geely Holding in July 2021, followed by a 99% equity interest acquisition in itself from Geely Auto and 1% from Geely Holding in August 2021.

Furthermore, Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., established in 2017 as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Geely Holding, focuses on R&D and production of essential NEV components, including electric powertrains and battery packs. In July 2021, ZEEKR entered a share purchase agreement to acquire a 51% equity interest in Ningbo Viridi, completing the transaction in October 2021. As a result, ZEEKR now holds a majority stake in Ningbo Viridi, which has been generating revenue since March 2019.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Ningbo Viridi’s research and development efforts are mainly focused on the following areas. • Battery pack. Ningbo Viridi innovates and pioneers a series of battery products and solutions for battery electric vehicles (BEVs), hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs), and plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs), featuring a high level of energy, convenience, and safety. For instance, Ningbo Viridi's batteries are onboard vehicles with a CLTC range of 546 km to 741 km, and their energy capacity can be replenished from 10% to 80% within approximately 30 minutes. In addition, Ningbo Viridi has developed the battery management system (BMS) to ensure performance, efficiency, and durability of battery packs. Based on AutoSar architecture and ISO26262, Ningbo Viridi’s BMS achieves the safety standard of ASIL C. Ningbo Viridi’s batteries and BMS have been deployed on a number of BEV brands within the Geely Group. • Motor and electric control. Ningbo Viridi is devoted to the research and development of motor and electric control products that offer high performance and efficiency, as well as low weight and improved noise, vibration, and harshness (NVH). Currently, the 200 kW motor of Ningbo Viridi has been deployed on the ZEEKR 009 and ZEEKR X, among other vehicle brands. • Charging solutions. Ningbo Viridi’s charging solution offers onboard charger solutions and charging pile solutions, such as the up-to-600 kW charging piles deployed in Ningbo Viridi's ultra charging stations.

title: Ningbo Viridi 
summary: Ningbo Viridi is dedicated to advancing innovation in battery technology and electric vehicle solutions, focusing on products for Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs), Hybrid Electric Vehicles (HEVs), and Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicles (PHEVs). Their cutting-edge battery packs offer impressive ranges of 546 km to 741 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in approximately 30 minutes. The company has also developed a Battery Management System (BMS) that meets AutoSar architecture and ISO26262 standards, achieving ASIL C safety certification, and is utilized by various brands within the Geely Group.

In addition to battery technology, Ningbo Viridi excels in high-performance motor and electric control systems, exemplified by their 200 kW motor featured in models such as the ZEEKR 009 and ZEEKR X. The company provides advanced charging solutions, including on-board chargers and high-capacity charging piles capable of delivering up to 600 kW, suitable for a variety of vehicles. Their charging infrastructure complies with international standards across regions like China, the U.S., Europe, Japan, and South Korea, and they are also innovating charging piles with liquid cooling capabilities.

As of December 31, 2023, Ningbo Viridi employs over 2,000 skilled professionals with expertise in the automotive and mechanical fields, underscoring their commitment to research and development as a cornerstone of their strategic goals and product enhancement.

title: Ningbo Viridi 
summary: Ningbo Viridi is a leader in research and development, focusing on enhancing its technological capabilities and product offerings in the electric vehicle sector. The company specializes in battery technology for Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs), Hybrid Electric Vehicles (HEVs), and Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicles (PHEVs), providing battery packs with energy capacities that allow for impressive ranges of 546 km to 741 km and rapid recharging capabilities. They are also innovating with liquid-cooled charging piles to further enhance performance. The ZEEKR 009 was the first mass-produced BEV model equipped with CATL’s Qilin battery, which gives the ZEEKR 009 an $822 \mathrm{km}$ maximum CLTC range. • The ZEEKR X is equipped with $66 \mathrm{kW h}$ battery packs, which enable the ZEEKR X a $560 \mathrm{km}$ or $512 \mathrm{km}$ maximum CLTC range depending on different vehicle versions. The battery packs developed by Ningbo Viridi can also be enhanced by Ningbo Viridi's proprietary battery management system, or BMS, to manage and optimize the battery performance, durability, and safety. metadata.title_summary:
title: Battery Solutions 
summary: ZEEKR vehicles are designed with a focus on reliable battery solutions that emphasize safety, efficiency, and durability. The ZEEKR 001 model offers versatile battery options, including 100 kWh and 86 kWh packs from CATL or Ningbo Viridi, with the 100 kWh version featuring Ni55 cells that provide a maximum CLTC range of 741 km. This battery can be charged from 10% to 80% in just 30 minutes, and a quick five-minute charge allows for about 120 km of travel. The 2024 model introduces 100 kWh or 95 kWh Qilin battery packs, with the 100 kWh version achieving a range of 750 km and enabling 210 km of travel with a five-minute charge. The ZEEKR 001 FR enhances performance further with an 800 V Qilin battery for improved range and charging speed.

The ZEEKR 009 offers larger battery options of 140 kWh or 116 kWh Qilin packs, also from CATL, achieving a remarkable maximum CLTC range of 822 km, marking it as the first mass-produced battery electric vehicle (BEV) utilizing this technology. Meanwhile, the ZEEKR X is equipped with a 66 kWh battery pack, providing a maximum CLTC range of 560 km or 512 km, depending on the version. Additionally, Ningbo Viridi's proprietary battery management system (BMS) enhances the performance, durability, and safety of its battery packs. For further details on Ningbo Viridi's innovations, refer to the relevant section on their technologies.
